mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to predict positive returns with > 50% accuracy over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving corporate output capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in consumer price inflation trigger anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 90th percentile of trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Divergence between payroll growth and unemployment decline yields no return predictability with t-stat below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently fail to correlate with subsequent equity returns with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals impending demand destruction and inventory gluts, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases with accuracy below 45%.
